hook.text: Granite is the backbone of every continent. Without it, there would be no dry land on Earth at all.

buildup.text: Granite is an intrusive igneous rock made mainly of quartz, feldspar, and mica, cooled deep underground.

discovery.text: Its large, visible crystals prove it cooled slowly over thousands of years inside magma chambers.

twist.text: Granite is less dense than oceanic basalt. That density difference is why continents float higher than ocean floors.

climax.text: Granite is durable, beautiful, and fundamental. It's used in buildings and monuments for its lasting strength.

quiz.question: Why do continents sit higher than ocean floors?
options: They are pushed up by wind
Granite is less dense than oceanic basalt
